test_detail: tests/B101-ingest-audit-verify.ps1 -- 45 offline static+logic-mirror checks pass, 0 fail; 2 optional live-HTTP cases SKIP (run by setting $env:M8_BASE_URL post-deploy). Node is absent on host, so the gate is pure PS 5.1 like the rest of the suite.
audit_finding: The brief's premise ('0 book nodes despite 4 sources; Step 2 never called') was PARTLY STALE. Live DB (project ltqpoupferwituusxwal) at build time: m8_knowledge_sources = 5 rows (ids 1,2,3,4,6; id 5 was deleted), and m8_graph_nodes = 161 nodes total, of which 20 trace to ingested sources. Per-source node counts: src1=8, src2=6, src3=5, src4=1 (near-dup of 3, mostly deduped), src6=0. So Step 2 DID run for sources 1-4; only source 6 (ingested 2026-06-20) is unextracted (0 nodes). The graph is NOT empty. Two real facts remain: (1) NONE of the 5 sources are books -- they are all tiny Collatz snippets (35-65 words); the Ibn Kathir / al-Bidaya wal-Nihaya books were never ingested even at Step 1, confirming m8-ingestion-empty-finding. (2) The recurring gap's root cause is that the 2-step pipeline has no atomicity, so Step 2 can be skipped, leaving 0-node sources (exactly source 6). Build-101 removes that failure mode: ingest-full does both steps in one call; ingest-extract-existing with no body would target source 6 and extract it.
lib_audit: lib/knowledge-intake.js audited, NOT changed (no bug found). (a) populateGraph() DOES write to m8_graph_nodes (db.from('m8_graph_nodes').insert, line ~237). (b) There is NO source_id column on m8_graph_nodes -- the column is source_doc_id (bigint), confirmed against the live information_schema; the re-extract endpoint joins on source_doc_id. (c) extractConcepts(source_id) works on an existing source_id -- it reads raw_text from m8_knowledge_sources by id.
notes: DEVIATIONS (all deliberate, for correctness): (1) Also committed vercel.json (4th file, beyond the listed 3). It is REQUIRED: for these plain (non-Next.js) Vercel functions the duration is read from vercel.json functions[], not from module.exports.config -- every long endpoint in the repo uses vercel.json, and upload-file.js's inline config is even dead code (wiped by the module.exports reassignment). Without the vercel.json entry the 180s would be a no-op and extraction would time out at the platform default. The inline module.exports.config = { maxDuration: 180 } is kept too (honors the brief, harmless) and is placed AFTER the handler assignment so the reassignment cannot wipe it. (2) The brief said populateGraph(candidates, { approve }); the real populateGraph signature takes ONLY candidates and ignores a 2nd arg -- passing the object would write ALL candidates regardless of tier (a bug). Instead the endpoints filter candidates by extraction_confidence caller-side, mirroring api/knowledge-extract.js. (3) Parallel-session race: HEAD moved during the build (c140547 -> 66cd57e) and 3 lib files (cost-profiles.js, intentClassifier.js, orchestrator.js) had uncommitted edits from another live session in the shared tree -- deliberately NOT staged (added only my 4 files explicitly, never -A). (4) Live repair NOT executed: deploying then POST /api/ingest-extract-existing (no body) will extract source 6 (uses Gemini + writes nodes) -- left for Muhammad to run post-deploy.
